docs.title: APEX Documentation
docs.text:
The Agricultural Policy Environmental eXtender. Field and small-watershed simulation of crop growth, hydrology, erosion, nutrients, and grazing. Plain-text inputs, plain-text outputs, open source.

Get started Downloads

  • Getting started

    Install APEX, run the JWRanch reference dataset, and learn the shape of a project folder.

  • Model theory

    Water balance, hydrology, crop growth, erosion and sediment, nutrient cycling, grazing.

  • Input reference

    Every input file: control, weather, soils, and management, with columns and units.

  • Output reference

    Annual, summary, and daily outputs, and what each column means.

  • Developer guide

    Build from source with CMake, run the test suite, and contribute.

  • Downloads

    Prebuilt binaries and the latest rolling build.

Work in progress

This documentation is a skeleton. Sections will be filled in over time.

docs.title: Build from source (CMake)
docs.text: APEX builds with CMake and presets. The default is Intel ifx, static, release. gfortran is also supported.

docs.title: Presets
docs.text:
{linux,windows,darwin}-{ifx,gfortran}-{release,debug}

ifx is x86_64 only, so macOS uses gfortran. See CMakePresets.json.

docs.title: Intel ifx (default)
docs.text:
source /opt/intel/oneapi/setvars.sh
cmake --preset linux-ifx-release
cmake --build --preset linux-ifx-release   # -> build/linux-ifx-release/apex

docs.title: gfortran
docs.text:
cmake --preset linux-gfortran-release
cmake --build --preset linux-gfortran-release

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• release links statically so the binary is self-contained; debug is dynamic and adds runtime checks.
  • The build needs Ninja and a Fortran compiler.

Work in progress

More detail (Windows, options) to come.

docs.title: Quick start: JWRanch
docs.text: The repository ships a small reference dataset, refdata/jwranch, a 10-year cow-calf ranch run for Bell County, Texas. It is the fastest way to confirm a working APEX build.

docs.title: Run it
docs.text:
From a built binary:

cd refdata/jwranch
/path/to/apex          # e.g. build/linux-ifx-release/apex

APEX reads APEXFILE.DAT (which lists the other inputs) and APEXRUN.DAT (the run list), then writes TXBELLEX_COW_CALF.* outputs and *.SUM into the working directory.

docs.title: Expected result
docs.text:
A successful run prints each simulated year and ends at:

    YEAR   10 OF   10

The annual crop yields land in TXBELLEX_COW_CALF.ACY, grazing in .AGZ, and the full report in .OUT.

Tip

Run in a copy of the folder so the generated outputs do not clutter the reference inputs.
